overview: The dependency graph is straightforward: `todo-api` has no dependencies and must be built first, while `todo-web` depends on `todo-api` and is built second. A Bottom-Up strategy is applied — foundation services are established before dependent consumers, ensuring the frontend can be developed and tested against a stable, fully-specified API contract.

phases.name: Foundation Services
phases.description: todo-api has no dependencies and forms the entire foundation layer. It must be implemented first so the frontend has a stable REST API to integrate against.

components.name: todo-api
responsibilities: Manage in-memory CRUD operations for tasks and categories using sync.RWMutex-protected maps
Expose RESTful HTTP endpoints for tasks and categories, including completion toggling and category-based filtering
Enforce data integrity rules — validate required fields, reject references to non-existent categories, and nullify categoryId on tasks when a category is deleted
Return consistent JSON responses with appropriate HTTP status codes and Content-Type headers
Enable CORS for all origins and expose a /health endpoint for operational readiness checks
components.architectureContext: This is the sole backend service in the system. It is called directly by the todo-web frontend via REST over HTTP. It has no outbound dependencies of its own. It listens on port 9090 and serves as the authoritative source of truth for all task and category data, held entirely in memory.
keyConsiderations: All shared state (tasks and categories maps) must be protected by sync.RWMutex — use RLock for reads and Lock for writes to avoid data races under concurrent requests
Deleting a category must iterate over all tasks and set their categoryId to nil atomically within a write lock to prevent partial updates
UUID generation via github.com/google/uuid must be the sole source of IDs — never accept client-supplied IDs for new resources
CORS middleware must set Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * and handle OPTIONS preflight requests before any route-specific logic runs
The multi-stage Dockerfile should use golang:1.22-alpine as builder and a minimal alpine (not scratch) final image to retain CA certificates and timezone data for time parsing
components.apiContract:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Todo API
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
  /categories:
    get:
      summary: List all categories
      responses:
        '200':
          description: List of categories
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: '#/components/schemas/Category'
    post:
      summary: Create a category
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: '#/components/schemas/CategoryInput'
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Created category
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Category'
  /categories/{id}:
    put:
      summary: Update a category
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: '#/components/schemas/CategoryInput'
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Updated category
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Category'
        '404':
          description: Not found
    delete:
      summary: Delete a category
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '204':
          description: Deleted
        '404':
          description: Not found
  /tasks:
    get:
      summary: List all tasks, optionally filtered by category
      parameters:
        - name: categoryId
          in: query
          required: false
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: List of tasks
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: '#/components/schemas/Task'
    post:
      summary: Create a task
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: '#/components/schemas/TaskInput'
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Created task
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Task'
  /tasks/{id}:
    put:
      summary: Update a task
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: '#/components/schemas/TaskInput'
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Updated task
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Task'
        '404':
          description: Not found
    delete:
      summary: Delete a task
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '204':
          description: Deleted
        '404':
          description: Not found
  /tasks/{id}/complete:
    patch:
      summary: Toggle task completion status
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                completed:
                  type: boolean
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Updated task
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Task'
        '404':
          description: Not found
components:
  schemas:
    Category:
      type: object
      properties:
        id:
          type: string
        name:
          type: string
        createdAt:
          type: string
          format: date-time
    CategoryInput:
      type: object
      required: [name]
      properties:
        name:
          type: string
    Task:
      type: object
      properties:
        id:
          type: string
        title:
          type: string
        completed:
          type: boolean
        dueDate:
          type: string
          format: date
          nullable: true
        categoryId:
          type: string
          nullable: true
        createdAt:
          type: string
          format: date-time
        updatedAt:
          type: string
          format: date-time
    TaskInput:
      type: object
      required: [title]
      properties:
        title:
          type: string
        dueDate:
          type: string
          format: date
          nullable: true
        categoryId:
          type: string
          nullable: true
        completed:
          type: boolean

phases.name: Frontend Application
phases.description: todo-web depends on todo-api being available. It is implemented second, consuming the fully-defined REST API from Phase 1 and delivering the complete user-facing single-page application.

components.name: todo-web
responsibilities: Render a responsive single-page application with a category sidebar and a task main panel, supporting full CRUD interactions for both tasks and categories
Communicate exclusively with todo-api via typed fetch wrappers in api.ts, using VITE_API_BASE_URL as the configurable base URL injected at build time
Manage all client-side state with React useState and useEffect, re-fetching data from the API after every mutation to keep the UI consistent
Visually highlight overdue incomplete tasks (dueDate < today) with a red due-date badge, and render completed tasks with a strikethrough style
Serve the compiled static assets via Nginx on port 9090, with SPA fallback routing and a /health endpoint returning HTTP 200
components.architectureContext: todo-web is the only client-facing component in the system. It depends entirely on todo-api for all data operations, communicating over REST/HTTP. No other component calls todo-web. Nginx acts as the static file server and health-check responder, while the React application running in the browser makes direct fetch calls to todo-api using the build-time-injected VITE_API_BASE_URL.
keyConsiderations: VITE_API_BASE_URL must be baked in at Docker build time via ARG/ENV in the Dockerfile and passed to `npm run build` — runtime injection is not possible with Vite's static build model without additional tooling

The nginx.conf must include a `try_files $uri $uri/ /index.html` fallback so that deep-linking and page refreshes resolve correctly for SPA routing, plus an explicit `/health` location block returning 200

Overdue detection must compare dueDate strings (YYYY-MM-DD) against the client's local date — use new Date() carefully to avoid UTC/local timezone off-by-one errors (parse as local noon, not midnight UTC)
Category deletion confirmation dialog must clearly communicate to the user that associated tasks will not be deleted but will become uncategorised, matching the backend's nullification behaviour
All API error responses should surface inline (not just console.error) — maintain an error state per operation and render user-readable messages beneath the relevant form or list item
components.apiContract:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Todo Web (Frontend)
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check served by Nginx
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
